symptom: 45s beauty product TVC completed with four continuous acts and native audio.
cause: Script used 10s hook, 15s proof, 10s application, 10s landing, each with entry/exit state and continuity anchors.
fix_pattern: For 45s product films, keep 3-4 long segments with explicit global arc and pass voice/BGM intent into Seedance native audio prompts.
anti_pattern: Do not split a 45s luxury TVC into too many tiny disconnected shots before render.

symptom: Four iteration rounds were created but all reused the same assets and score stayed at 0.713.
cause: The iterate command records scorecards and decisions but does not call prompt mutation, render, compare, and replacement.
fix_pattern: Implement executable iteration: choose low-score segment targets, rewrite prompts from gate feedback, render candidates, compare against current best, and replace only improved clips.
anti_pattern: Do not count scorecard-only rounds as generation iteration.

symptom: Video and storyboard gates produced brittle scores because they could not inspect actual frames.
cause: Professional gate currently relies on JSON text heuristics for product recognizability, motion, aesthetics, and continuity.
fix_pattern: Extract representative frames/contact sheets and run a multimodal reviewer that writes evidence and segment-level regenerate targets into gate JSON.
anti_pattern: Do not use clip URL text or prompt keywords as a substitute for visual QA.

symptom: HyperFrames dry-run created a composition but real render against the remote 45s MP4 timed out.
cause: The caption renderer invoked HyperFrames directly on a remote source without localizing media or enforcing a reliable fallback path.
fix_pattern: Download/localize source video before HyperFrames render, stream logs with a bounded timeout, and fall back to ffmpeg subtitle burn-in when render exceeds SLA.
anti_pattern: Do not block the whole video pipeline on an unbounded HyperFrames render.
